message:
fix(fcp-sdk/ratelimit): atomic-rename + I/O-lock checkpoint persist (br-ogeov)

Closes flywheel_connectors-ogeov: cross-crate sweep c46eb434 moved
RateLimitCheckpointStore::persist_file outside the pools.write() lock to
reduce I/O contention but added no replacement serialization. Two
concurrent try_consume calls could race on File::create(path) (truncate
+ write, no tempfile/rename), producing torn or interleaved bytes that
fail JSON parse on next startup and silently drop all rate-limit state.

Fix follows the same atomic-rename pattern already used in
fcp-store/durable.rs:1355 and fcp-host/admin_state.rs:4889:

- RateLimitCheckpointStore now carries an Arc<Mutex<()>> io_lock that
  serializes the write+rename critical section so only one writer
  touches the target path at a time.
- persist_file writes to a unique temp file (file_name + pid + nanos +
  retry suffix) via OpenOptions::create_new, fsyncs it, then renames
  over self.path. The temp file is removed on any failure path.
- open_unique_checkpoint_temp_file mirrors durable.rs's
  open_unique_snapshot_temp_file shape (32 retries on AlreadyExists).

Regression test concurrent_try_consume_keeps_checkpoint_file_valid_json
spins 16 writer threads x 250 try_consume calls each against one shared
RateLimitTracker while a reader thread continuously parses the
checkpoint file. The reader must always see valid JSON of the expected
shape, and the final on-disk total (prev + curr) must equal exactly
THREADS * ITERS, proving no writes were lost in the storm.

Verification:
- 114 / 114 fcp-sdk lib::ratelimit tests pass under the new test.
- cargo clippy -p fcp-sdk --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings clean
  (--no-deps because an unrelated agent has uncommitted SchemaId
  changes in fcp-cbor that fail the clippy doc-panics gate; not in
  scope for this bead).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
